meta.title: Auto and Home Insurance Sales
meta.catalog: Guidewire PolicyCenter — Agent-Channel Personal Lines Catalog

steps.output: Prospect record created in CRM; source code recorded

steps.activity: Initial contact made and appointment scheduled
steps.output: Contact within 1 business day of lead assignment; appointment confirmed
Current insurer, coverage gaps, claims history and risk characteristics; notes in CRM
Bundled auto + home discount opportunity presented if applicable

steps.activity: Application data entered into agent portal
steps.output: Vehicle/property, personal and claims history entered; address verified
Instant quote for auto-accept; referral cases routed to Underwriting

steps.activity: Quote presented and explained to prospect
steps.output: Benefits, exclusions, excess and payment options explained per disclosure obligations
Decision within 2 business hours; agent notified of outcome and revised terms

steps.activity: Prospect agrees; application finalised and signed
steps.output: Application signed; agent confirms scope of cover and prospect understanding
Payment processed via agent portal; receipt issued
Policy number generated; coverage effective from agreed start date
Schedule, certificate and PDS dispatched within 2 business days
Policy number, premium, product type and commission logged

swim_phases.name: Prospect & needs analysis
swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 1–4 · source to bundle
swim_phases.note: Leads are contacted within a day; needs analysis identifies coverage gaps and auto+home bundling opportunities.

swim_phases.name: Quotation
swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 5–8 · rate, present, underwrite
swim_phases.note: The rating engine auto-accepts or refers; high-risk applications are held for written underwriting approval before any bind.

swim_phases.name: Conversion & issuance
swim_phases.subtitle: Steps 9–13 · finalise to fulfil
swim_phases.note: On agreement the application is signed and paid, the policy issues, documents dispatch, and the sale is logged in CRM.

fitgap.summary_line: Overall alignment of SOP-INS-008 against the Guidewire PolicyCenter agent-channel catalog
detail_slides: Steps 1–4 vs Guidewire PolicyCenter agent catalog
Steps 5–10 vs Guidewire PolicyCenter agent catalog
Steps 11–13 vs Guidewire PolicyCenter agent catalog

items.name: Lead capture & contact SLA
items.detail: CRM lead capture with a 1-day contact SLA aligns with PolicyCenter agent lead management.

items.name: Needs analysis
items.detail: Needs are discussed and noted, but not captured as a structured, auditable suitability record as the catalog requires for conduct compliance.

items.name: Multi-product cross-sell
items.detail: Auto+home bundling assessment aligns with PolicyCenter account-rounding/cross-sell prompts.

items.name: Agent ready-to-sell enforcement
items.detail: Licensing is a stated prerequisite but not enforced at point of sale. PolicyCenter blocks quote/bind for agents not currently licensed and appointed for the product/territory.

items.name: Application capture & address verify
items.detail: Portal capture with address verification aligns with PolicyCenter submission intake.

items.name: Risk-data enrichment
items.detail: Rating uses entered data only. PolicyCenter enriches with property/flood, VIN and prior-claims data (and a telematics option for auto) for pricing accuracy and anti-selection control.

items.name: Auto-accept rules engine
items.detail: Rules-based auto-accept present, but no predictive risk-model score to widen straight-through binding.

items.name: Disclosure at quote
items.detail: Benefits, exclusions and excess explained per disclosure obligations align with PolicyCenter conduct controls.

items.name: Underwriting referral & SLAs
items.detail: 2-hour referral with revised-terms handling aligns with PolicyCenter referral workflow.

items.name: KYC / application-fraud screening
items.detail: No KYC/sanctions or application-fraud screening at sale; misrepresentation is only handled post-issuance. PolicyCenter screens and fraud-scores applications before binding.

items.name: Signed application & e-signature
items.detail: Application is 'digital or paper'; the catalog mandates an auditable e-signature with a captured disclosure acknowledgement before issuance.

items.name: Payment & instant issuance
items.detail: Portal payment and instant issuance with effective date align with PolicyCenter billing and issuance.

items.name: Document dispatch & sale logging
items.detail: 2-day document dispatch and CRM sale logging align with PolicyCenter fulfilment and reporting.

critical_missing.title: Agent ready-to-sell enforcement
critical_missing.detail: Licensing is a stated prerequisite but not enforced in the portal. The catalog blocks quote/bind for agents not currently licensed and appointed for the product and territory (links to SOP-INS-005).

critical_missing.title: Third-party risk-data enrichment
critical_missing.detail: Rating relies on entered data only, with no property/flood, VIN or prior-claims enrichment and no telematics option for auto — a pricing-accuracy and anti-selection gap.

critical_missing.title: KYC / application-fraud screening
critical_missing.detail: No identity/sanctions or application-fraud screening at sale; misrepresentation is only addressed after issuance, increasing fraud and conduct exposure.

critical_missing.title: Mandatory e-signature & suitability
critical_missing.detail: Application acceptance is 'digital or paper' with no mandated e-signature or structured suitability record, a conduct and dispute exposure.

control_bars.name: Ready-to-sell
control_bars.note: Licensing not enforced at point of sale

remediations.title: Enforce agent ready-to-sell at point of sale
remediations.detail: Block quote/bind for agents not currently licensed and appointed for the product/territory, integrated with the licensing system (SOP-INS-005). Effort: 3 weeks.

remediations.title: Enrich rating with risk data & telematics
remediations.detail: Append property/flood, VIN and prior-claims data and offer a telematics rating option for auto. Effort: 6 weeks (data partners).

remediations.title: Add KYC / application-fraud screening
remediations.detail: Screen identity/sanctions and fraud-score applications before binding; route hits to Compliance. Effort: 4 weeks.

remediations.title: Mandate e-signature & structured suitability
remediations.detail: Require an auditable e-signature with captured disclosure acknowledgement and a structured suitability record before issuance. Effort: 3 weeks.

remediations.title: Add predictive risk scoring
remediations.detail: Layer a predictive risk-model score over the rules engine to widen straight-through binding for low-risk applications. Effort: 5 weeks.

opt_steps.output: Prospect record in CRM; source code
Quote/bind enabled only if agent is currently licensed and appointed
Contact within 1 business day; appointment confirmed
Auditable suitability record captured; coverage gaps identified
Bundled auto+home discount presented if applicable
Property/flood, VIN and prior-claims data appended; address verified
Identity/sanctions screened and application fraud-scored before binding

opt_steps.activity: Rating engine quote with predictive score
opt_steps.output: Rules plus predictive score widen straight-through binding; refer otherwise
Benefits, exclusions, excess and payment options per disclosure
Decision within 2 business hours; revised terms communicated
Opt-in usage-based rating offered for auto with safe-driving discount
Auditable e-signature and disclosure acknowledgement captured
Policy number; coverage effective from start date
Schedule, certificate, PDS within 2 business days
Policy number, premium, product and commission logged

optimised_doc.subtitle: Optimised per Fit-Gap Analysis against the Guidewire PolicyCenter Agent-Channel Catalog

blocks: OPTIMISATION SUMMARY — What changed in v3.0

This version incorporates 5 remediation actions from Fit-Gap Report FG-INS-008. Alignment to the Guidewire PolicyCenter agent-channel catalog increased from 68% to 91%. Critical additions: agent ready-to-sell enforcement, third-party risk-data enrichment with telematics, KYC/application-fraud screening, mandatory e-signature with structured suitability, and predictive risk scoring.
NEW STEP (green) — step added to close a gap vs the PolicyCenter catalog
ENHANCED (amber) — existing step updated with additional controls or automation

sections.title: 1. Purpose
blocks: This Standard Operating Procedure defines the process for quoting and converting prospects into auto and home policyholders through the Meridian agent channel. Version 3.0 incorporates optimisations from fit-gap analysis against the Guidewire PolicyCenter Agent-Channel Personal Lines Catalog, achieving 91% alignment (up from 68% in v2.2).

sections.title: 2. Scope
blocks: This procedure applies to agent-sourced and inbound auto and home enquiries, new business, cross-sales and account rounding, via the agent portal or telephony channel.
Scope extended: a ready-to-sell gate and KYC/application-fraud screening now apply to every agent-assisted sale before binding.
This procedure does NOT cover online direct sales (SOP-INS-001), commercial vehicle fleets, or landlord insurance beyond standard residential.

sections.title: 4. Roles and Responsibilities
blocks: Identifies prospects; runs needs/suitability; presents options; completes and e-signs application.
Reviews referrals; sets non-standard terms; approves high-risk cases in writing.
Owns KYC/sanctions and application-fraud dispositions and misrepresentation handling.
Handles inbound enquiries and channel-assisted applications.

sections.title: 5. Readiness & Screening (New Section — v3.0)
blocks: New section. Required by the PolicyCenter catalog. A ready-to-sell gate and KYC/application-fraud screening now precede any bind.
Identity and watchlist screened before bind

sections.title: 6. Process Steps
blocks: Rating engine quote with predictive score

sections.title: 7. Exception Handling
blocks: Agent records declination with reason code in CRM; follow-up task set for 30 days.
If price was the objection, the agent may request a pricing review where the risk profile warrants an exception.
Applications outside standard guidelines are held for underwriting review before any verbal commitment.
The agent must not communicate binding confirmation until written underwriting approval is received.
Application-fraud scoring now flags likely misrepresentation before binding, not only after issuance.
Pre-bind: a high fraud score routes the application to Compliance before any cover is bound.
Post-issuance: material misrepresentation is notified to Underwriting and Compliance within 1 business day.
Compliance determines whether the policy is void or voidable and advises required action.

sections.title: 8. Systems and Tools
blocks: Policy record, documents, endorsements, ready-to-sell gate
Premium calculation, enrichment, predictive scoring, telematics

sections.title: 9. Key Controls and Audit Points
blocks: Controls expanded in v3.0: ready-to-sell enforcement, risk-data enrichment, KYC/fraud screening, e-signature and predictive scoring.
Ready-to-sell gate: no quote/bind by an unlicensed or unappointed agent.
Risk-data enrichment: property/flood, VIN and claims data improve pricing.
KYC/fraud screening: identity, sanctions and fraud scored before binding.
E-signature: audited acceptance with disclosure acknowledgement before issuance.
Suitability: structured needs/suitability record captured for conduct compliance.
Predictive scoring: rules-engine binding refined by a governed risk model.
Immutable audit trail: needs, screening, quote, acceptance and issuance events logged.

sections.title: 11. Document Control
blocks: Aligned to revised product disclosure obligations; updated payment gateway steps
OPTIMISED: ready-to-sell enforcement; risk-data enrichment & telematics; KYC/fraud screening; mandatory e-signature & suitability; predictive scoring; catalog alignment 68% → 91%
